properties.name: Description
properties.value:
**Lore of the Arcane**

Magic is said to be the driving force of the entropy of the universe, the source of instability of laws of nature. If harnessed correctly, it can grant physical strength, emotional connection, mental focus, spiritual alignment, energetic vitalization, and all sorts of miraculous feats. Yet to harness such powers demands disciplined hard work.

Practitioners have identified many different aspects to cast spells with arcane magic. To become a powerful practitioner of arcane magic, any aspiring arcanist needs to be dynamic and harmonious with at least some of the aspects of the arcane. Spellcasters with differing origins may also tap into several aspects of the arcane.

**Aspects of Arcane**

**Abjuration** school is dedicated to protective magic, the magic that cancels or interferes with other kinds of magic, supernatural energies and effects through twisting the reality’s natural protection against arcane.

**Alteration** and **Transmutation** schools are dedicated to the skill to transform one form of matter into another, using magic to transform the molecular nature of matter.

**Conjuration** and **Summoning** schools are dedicated to spontaneous transportation or manifesting of creatures, energies, objects into reality, by tapping into a wizard's creative energy and twisting the flow of time and space.

**Divination** school is dedicated to acquiring or exposing information about the unknown and the future. Arcanists achieve this feat by not pulling knowledge from the future itself, but through manifesting an otherwise unexplorable wisdom hidden in their minds. However, existence of other diviners within reach may cloud the manifestation capabilities of diviners.

**Enchantment** or **Charm** schools are dedicated to beguiling the minds of others to make them do the things you want. Such a feat is accomplished by manipulating the sensory perceptions and the nervous system of the victim through the use of arcane abilities.

**Evocation** school is dedicated to creating matter and energy out of nothing by forcing reality into submission, and altering the nature of the elements existing near the caster.

**Illusion** and **Phantasm** schools are dedicated to changing the appearance of something by manifesting images, hallucinations and deception. The magic achieves the desired effect by manipulating matters’ visibility through arcane.

**Necromancy** is dedicated to spells which involve death, undeath and the manipulation of the energy of life. There are many methods of casting necromantic spells, such as manipulating the time flow in a single object, or a body, to rewind death and injury.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
**Rules of Divinity**

The ancient tablets, parchments and scrolls on divine magic written by the priests of old describe divine magic as a solemn understanding between a greater power and a mortal being. They describe this relationship as a symbiosis: the greater power rejoices the faith of the believer and in return provides the miracles to one in need. However, some arcane researchers dwell on an entirely different idea that these pious people take their power to create miracles only through their willpower and creed. In either case, their miracles can be used to heal the wounded, bless the miserable, return sight to the sightless and guide people through hardships. They appear in history as miracle workers, prodigies of the holy, and wielders of the scripture.

Divine magic is direct, predictable, and easy to accept when viewed by an educated witness in the matters of the occult. It may be holy or dark, benign or catastrophic, depending on the person performing the miracles. If a believer somehow loses her faith or starts to walk in a completely different path, it is said that the greater power either curses her or stops granting miracles to her. More credible researchers interpose this idea by saying that the loss of power is due to the caster losing belief in themselves.

**Origin of Divinity**

The knowledge passed from generation to generation on divine magic describes its origin as such: “It is granted by a beneficent, abusive, or egoistical greater power bearing the capability to grant miracles.” Some researchers contradict this idea by dictating that the origin of divine magic comes from the very souls each person possesses. Research made on rural areas brings into our view that in some rare cases divine power appears in cases like prolonged delusion, abuse of hallucinogenic mushrooms, and even manifests under mental duress too. Throughout history the world has seen many different cults, beliefs, and religions supporting many different approaches to the origin of their own powers, and who can say which idea is correct?

Holy offerings, incense, and holy symbols are the usual offerings for divine spells, but in some cases, it has been seen that some acolytes pray upon profane religions offering live sacrifices as well.

**The Never-ending Path**

In Historica Arcanum, the work of a pious character never ends. The belief in the existence of celestial concepts took a hit among the masses during the Industrial Age. The practitioners of the divine powers are quite rare, and mundane people do not have the educated eye to notice them in more modern times. There may be trouble in following the path of the pious, but it is a path that is worth following.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
**Fiery Blood**

Some magic isn't earned by hard work, endless hours of study or understanding of the arcane; some magic is inherent in the blood passed from many ancestors and received by the caster. In this aspect, the ancestor in question is mostly a creature or concept which has great power and spellcasting prowess. These kinds of spellcasters use the raw magic which burns in their veins. Such people bear a magical birthright descended to them from a bizarre or alien bloodline, some kind of otherworldly force or manifestation of a cosmic energy in them. These casters don't choose to be such, they are chosen to be as they are.

**Raw Potential**

Practitioners of inherent magic are rare to come by. They are always in a quest to unlock their raw potential or tap into the unknown limits of their powers. Such people soon discover that their power never stays dormant once awoken.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
**Authentic Artist**

Some magic manifests under the powers of song, art, or speech. This kind of arcane power is a lot more subtle. The performers of this kind of magic can inspire, arouse, excite, hearten, impress, influence, motivate, and provoke by the use of their magical song, inspirational speech or gorgeous art. It is said that the art of these magics is the reflection of words used during the creation of the universe; thus, the performers of such magic benefit from various kinds of different magics. In other words, this kind of magic is mostly based on art, stories, music and oratory skills and involves knowing a little bit from everything at a time but never grasping one subject wholly.

**Potent Troubadour**

This kind of magic can be used a lot more subtly compared with many other types. It can be hidden behind poems, songs, musical notes or even behind speeches. It is really easy for such a caster to inspire allies or cripple foes, alter how people think, cause confusion or create deception.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
**Apostle of Terra Firma**

The magic of nature is perhaps the most pure of occult arts, yet it hosts great irony as you twist the laws of nature using nature itself. Texts passed down on obelisks, standing stones and other archaic information detail this kind of magic as a connection between forces of nature and a mortal capable of twisting the laws of nature to unleash the force. These arcane powers are harsh, raw and less refined than other aspects.

Nature magic is severe, sharp, acute, and to the point, as nature herself. Those with an educated eye and mind will perceive that this kind of magic rebounds on flora and fauna in a miniscule way.

**Roots of Augury**

According to the combined observations of and insight of sages through the ages, the amassed knowledge on nature magic describes it as such: ”The archaic forces of mother nature are granted by the so-called “ancient spirits of nature” to a select people who hear a call to the wilds. These ancient spirits are most likely a product of a person’s subconsciousness born with the inherent power of nature magic. The ways of these spellcasters are primitive and rigid as are the ways of nature.”

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
**Bonding Contracts**

The first users of the arcane were wizards, their powers and rebounds on the world gave birth to sorcerers. The combined magic in turn spawned beings of immense arcane instability, who can grant powers through pacts. Some arcanists seeking power or ways to fulfill their desires forge contracts with these patrons. In the dusty pages of history, magic influenced many supernatural creatures and allowed them to inflict great changes among the common folk. If one being’s desire and a supernatural being’s agenda fit, these contracts give them magical powers, allowing them to use magic even though they are not wizards who study the arcane, or sorcerers that inherit a great power.

**Invoking Arcana**

Creatures gain the ability to cast spells through pacts, and their bonding terms also invoke the mysterious powers in the arcane boundaries of magic. Their eldritch abilities seem mysterious even in the eyes of arcane masters. The chains of the pact makers may be broken if the creature on the receiving end reaches the peaks of mystic arcanum.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: When you cast this spell, you fall asleep and can dream up to six humanoids you have seen before. You can share your memories with those humanoids through dreams. The creatures dream about your selected memories in their sleep and remember it when they wake up. They experience the memories as if watching from the side, rather than directly in your perspective. You can share your emotions.

properties.name: Components
properties.value: V, S, M (wine, essence, or purified water)

properties.name: Description
properties.value: When you cast this spell, you choose up to five creatures within range. You bolster the creatures you choose with holy resolve. Each creature you choose gains advantage against all saving throws against poison and disease; they also gain 10 temporary hit points for the duration of this spell.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: For the duration, you sense the presence of any Djinn or Djinn-possessed vessels within 30 feet of you. If you sense any Djinni in this way, you can use your action to see a faint aura around any visible creature in the area to determine its type. The spell can penetrate most barriers, but it is blocked by 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of common metal, of thin sheet of lead, or 3 feet of wood or dirt.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
You tap into the dark power of Al-Ghaib to utter twisted words that spread madness. You choose a creature within range. They must succeed on a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save they will get one of the following effects, and they will take 3d6 psychic damage. You choose one of the effects below:
• The target will lose the ability to speak for 1 minute. When it tries to talk, it will utter the names of the great djinn lords.
• The target's speed is reduced to 0 until the end of your next turn.
• The target cannot use reactions.
• The target will have disadvantage on all its ability checks for 1 hour.
On a success, they only take half damage and suffer no other ill effects.

properties.name: Components
properties.value: V, S, M (A vial of consecrated water)

properties.name: Duration
properties.value: Concentration, up to 10 minutes or until the Djinn is Exorcized

properties.name: Description
properties.value: You focus on a Djinni-possessed vessel and force the Djinni to lose its Al-Ghaib form and its grip on the vessel. The Djinn rolls a Constitution save with disadvantage each turn. On a failure, the Djinn returns to its ethereal form. If it makes the saving throw, then you may choose to force the Djinni to repeat the saving throw on your next turn. To fully exorcize the Djinn, it must either be convinced to leave the possessed body, or must be defeated in combat outside of its possessed vessel.

properties.name: At Higher Levels
properties.value: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level the exorcism will affect a Zawbaw'ah. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level the exorcism will affect a Nar-as Samum or a Shamhuresh.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Your hands glow and you utter words from your holy scripture, summoning a faint aura of blessing that covers an ally of your choosing. Your ally will make the next attack roll, saving throw or skill check with advantage.

properties.name: At Higher Levels
properties.value: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the recipients of this spell will increase for each slot level above 1st.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: When you cast this spell, choose up to three creatures with whom you have a direct, unobscured line of sight within 30 ft. They make a Constitution saving throw. They take 5d10 necrotic damage on a failed save and half as much on a successful one. Upon failing the saving throw, any creature which is not undead or a construct becomes frightened. A creature can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. On a failed save and if the creature dies, the creature will raise as an undead under your control for the duration of 1 minute.

properties.name: At Higher Levels
properties.value: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, each slot above 4th will increase the targets of the spell by 1 and damage taken by 1d10 necrotic damage.

properties.name: Components
properties.value: V, S, M (an eye of a cat)

properties.name: Description
properties.value: You cut the cat eye in half and utter the accursed words of the spell, and a powerful being of demonic nature will answer. You ask a single question concerning a specific goal, event, or activity to occur within 7 days. The GM must offer a truthful reply. The reply might be a short phrase, a cryptic rhyme, or an omen. The spell doesn't take into account any possible circumstances that might change the outcome, such as the casting of additional spells or the loss or gain of a companion.

properties.name: At Higher Levels
properties.value: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, each slot above 4th will increase the time of the occurrence by 7 days and the number of questions by 1, but for each slot over 4th the caster will gain 1 level of exhaustion.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
You attempt to twist the mind of a humanoid you can see within 10 ft. of you. The target makes a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or suffers one effect you choose from below:
• You choose some specific information from the target's mind and erase it completely. This information can be the target's name, a language, a book it read, an event it saw, etc.
• You plant new memories instead of the old ones. You can change 1 minute of the target's memory with any memory you would like.
The victim suffers from violent fits when trying to recall their altered memories, such as repeatedly hitting their head.

properties.name: Components
properties.value: S, M (a string of black cat fur)

properties.name: Duration
properties.value: One day, or until the knot is broken

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
You knot the fur string to make a sign of supernatural hex. You create one of the following magical effects within range:
• The target's fertility drops to 0.
• The target suffers from bad luck in all ordinary aspects of life.
• The target suffers from random mundane mishaps.

properties.name: Components
properties.value: V, S, M (a piece of old parchment with Arabic runes written on it by black ink)

properties.name: Description
properties.value: You burn the paper, utter the words of the spell, and inhale the smoke to tap into the wisdom of Avicenna. You gain information on how the human body works. For ten minutes, you gain advantage on Medicine checks and detect any natural disease and poison harming the body. Although the existence of poison and disease can be detected, the natures of both will require a medicine check with proper difficulties.

properties.name: Components
properties.value: V, M (a silver mirror worth 25 gp which the spell consumes)

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
You shatter the silvery mirror with your hand and crumble the shards. Amidst the crackling sound, you utter the truthful words of the sophist Rumi.
You affect any creature that can hear your voice. All the creatures need to make a Charisma saving throw, and on a failed save, every creature that shape changed by magic or skill will forcefully revert back to its original form. Every illusion spell that shows the target as someone or something else and every transmutation spell that changes the target's shape will be broken. This spell only works on sentient creatures; it does not work on objects.

properties.name: Components
properties.value: S, M (A small Nazar bead worth 1 cp which the spell consumes)

properties.name: Description
properties.value: You crush the Nazar bead in your hand and blow the dust at your target, a creature of your choice within 30 ft. That creature makes a Wisdom saving throw or makes the next ability check or attack roll with disadvantage.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
Your eyes morph to the colors of the Evil Eye, momentarily, and you curse your target, a creature of your choice within 30 ft. That creature will be affected by one effect of your choice:
• The target must make a Strength saving throw or gain one level of exhaustion (If the target already has one, this will not have an effect)
• The target must make a Dexterity saving throw or gain disadvantage on any one task that needs fine manipulation.
• The target must make a Constitution saving throw or lose the use of hit dice during the next short rest.
• The target must make an Intelligence saving throw or lose the ability to read for one hour.
• The target must make a Wisdom saving throw or gain disadvantage on the next spell save it makes.
• The target must make a Charisma saving throw or gain disadvantage on the next Persuasion, Intimidation, Deception or Performance check.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
You blow out a minor boon, a sign of supernatural blessing. You create one of the following magical effects within range:
• The target’s fertility chance increases.
• The target has good luck in all ordinary aspects of life.
• If the target has a hex or a curse placed on itself, the spell offers ways to undo this curse or hex.

properties.name: Components
properties.value: V, S, M (A piece of silk worth 250 gp which the spell consumes)

properties.name: Description
properties.value: By magically letting go of the chains that bind you to this world, your body transcends its limitations. You gain resistance against all damage types except for psychic, which you gain immunity against.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
Choose a creature within 30 ft. That creature must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save you learn its past sinful actions. The GM will decide what entails a sin depending on your character’s own beliefs. You learn the sins as a vision where you see through the eyes of the sinner. The most recent sin is the most vivid one; however, as you delve deeper, the visions will get hazy - which require a further casting of the spell. The creature is not aware of the spell the first time.
If you cast this spell on the same creature again, you can probe deep into the older sins. The creature will realize someone has reached into their minds with magic in the second casting of the spell. On the third time, they will know who is probing into their minds, but you will be able to choose a direct sin from the creature’s mind (You can ask questions, like “What is your biggest sin?” “What is the sin you regret the most?” etc.).

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Whenever you need to deceive, you may use the Shroud of Taqiyya to help out in your lies, by crumbling the stale bread between your fingers with arcane hand gestures. You will have advantage on Deception skill checks, and you also gain advantage on saving throws against any spells such as *zone of truth*.

properties.name: At Higher Levels
properties.value: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, each slot above 2nd will increase the duration by 1 hour.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: You call down smokeless white flames of Solomon, the Sultan of the Djinn, from Al-Ghaib, spreading in a 60 ft. area. You have to choose a resistance that an enemy creature possesses. Any creature within the area will have to roll a Constitution saving throw or lose that resistance and also take 2d8 of that kind of damage each of their turn for the duration of the spell.

properties.name: At Higher Levels
properties.value: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, each slot above 5th will increase the targets of the spell by 1.

properties.name: Components
properties.value: V, S, M (a reverse text from any holy book, written in spirals on a piece of blood soaked papyrus)

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
As you chant a holy text in reverse, the papyrus in your hand starts glowing an ominous hue. Reality twists and turns as the papyrus consumes itself, summoning beings from Al-Ghaib.
You summon 1 Nar-as Samum or Shamhuresh that appears in an unoccupied space you can see within range. Any djinni immediately returns to Al-Ghaib when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends. The summoned djinn are hostile against all except for any natives of the Al-Ghaib. Roll initiative for the summoned djinni. Djinni will attack the creatures they are hostile towards to the best of their ability, prioritizing targets closer to them.
As a part of casting the spell, you can offer a live sacrifice? and form a circle with blood. This will let you form a circle that the summoned djinni can’t pass, or inflict any kind of harm to the creatures on the other side of the circle. Djinni summoned this way can’t use their Form of the Ghaib and Possession abilities.

properties.name: At Higher Levels
properties.value: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, each slot above 6th will summon another Nar-as Samum, or Shamhuresh.

properties.name: Components
properties.value: V, S, M (a reverse text from any holy book, written on a piece of papyrus)

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
As you chant a holy text in reverse, the papyrus in your hand starts glowing an ominous hue. Reality twists and turns as the papyrus consumes itself, summoning beings from Al-Ghaib.
You summon 2 djinns that are CR 1 or one djinn that is CR 2 of your choice that appear in unoccupied spaces you can see within range. Any djinni immediately returns to Al-Ghaib when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends. The summoned djinn are hostile against all except for any natives of the Al-Ghaib. Roll initiative for the summoned djinni as a group. Djinni will attack the creatures they are hostile towards to the best of their ability, prioritizing targets closer to them.
As a part of casting the spell, you can offer a live sacrifice? and form a circle with blood. This will let you form a circle that the summoned djinni can’t pass, or inflict any kind of harm to the creatures on the other side of the circle. Djinni summoned this way can’t use their Form of the Ghaib and Possession abilities.

properties.name: Components
properties.value: V, S, M (a reverse text from any holy book, carved in spirals on a bloodsoaked obsidian)

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
As you chant a holy text in reverse, the papyrus in your hand starts glowing an ominous hue. Reality twists and turns as the papyrus consumes itself, summoning beings from Al-Ghaib.
You summon 1 noble djinni of your choice (Abu Al Aswad, Abu Al Harid, Abu Mihriz, Abu Said) that appears in an unoccupied space you can see within range. Any djinni immediately returns to Al-Ghaib when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends. The summoned djinn are hostile against all except for any natives of the Al-Ghaib. Roll initiative for the summoned djinni. Djinni will attack the creatures they are hostile towards to the best of their ability, prioritizing targets closer to them.
As a part of casting the spell, you can offer a live sacrifice? and form a circle with blood. This will let you form a circle that the summoned djinni can’t pass, or inflict any kind of harm to the creatures on the other side of the circle. Djinni summoned this way can’t use their Form of the Ghaib and Possession abilities.

properties.name: Components
properties.value: V, S, M (a reverse text from any holy book, written in spirals on a papyrus)

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
As you chant a holy text in reverse, the papyrus in your hand starts glowing an ominous hue. Reality twists and turns as the papyrus consumes itself, summoning beings from Al-Ghaib.
You summon 1 Zawbaw’ah that appears in an unoccupied space you can see within range. Any djinni immediately returns to Al-Ghaib when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends. The summoned djinn are hostile against all except for any natives of the Al-Ghaib. Roll initiative for the summoned djinni. Djinni will attack the creatures they are hostile towards to the best of their ability, prioritizing targets closer to them.
As a part of casting the spell, you can offer a live sacrifice? and form a circle with blood. This will let you form a circle that the summoned djinni can’t pass, or inflict any kind of harm to the creatures on the other side of the circle. Djinni summoned this way can’t use their Form of the Ghaib and Possession abilities.

properties.name: At Higher Levels
properties.value: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, each slot above 4th will summon another Zawbaw’ah.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: You let loose a chant of your faith, altering the reality around you with the power of your belief. Any friend and foe who is in hearing distance will be affected. Each foe in the area must make a Wisdom saving throw. It takes 10d8 psychic damage and becomes paralyzed on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Any of the targets can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Any allies in the area of effect will gain the same exact amount (of the original damage) as temporary hit points for 1 minute.

properties.name: Components
properties.value: V, S, M (a piece of armor or armament that has been blessed by a priest)

properties.name: Description
properties.value: You choose one creature and yourself. Both of you add your proficiency bonuses to the damage of your next successful melee, ranged or spell attack.

properties.name: At Higher Levels
properties.value: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3th level or higher, each slot above 2nd will increase the targets of the spell by 1.

properties.name: Attunement Requirement
properties.value: by a wizard, warlock or sorcerer

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
Al-Jazari was a scientist from the court of Saladin. He was known as a man of great education and knowledge. This book was one of the very few books written by Al-Jazari and can be used as a wizard's spellbook and arcane focus at the same time. The book contains these spells:
• grease • fog cloud • unseen servant • web • sleet storm • stinking cloud
The attuned wizard gains these spells as if they are scribed into the spellbook and can memorize them. The wizard also gets +1 to all saving throws versus Conjuration magic.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Justinian the Great is considered by many to be the most capable Basileos of the Byzantine Empire. It is rumored that he never slept throughout his reign. It is this amulet that gave him the ability to do so. The wearer requires no sleep and is immune to any kind of magic or spell-like abilities that would put it to sleep. However, the wearer still needs to mentally or physically rest for 8 hours in order to benefit from long rest.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: These Turkish coffee cups are used for fortune telling. The arcane residue from the divining spells sometimes remain on the cups themselves. These cups are quite common in the arcane circles and grant the user the ability to cast guidance once, and after that they are regular coffee cups. It also can be used for fortune-telling feats and features.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: This crown is made from gold and small pieces of diamond are inlaid as thorns. It grants the wearer resistance against piercing damage. The wearer also has advantage on all Wisdom saving throws. The crown also gives the ability to cast telepathic bond once a day. This ability recharges at dawn.

properties.name: Attunement Requirement
properties.value: by a creature with at least 19 Charisma

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
This is the crown of the very first sovereign of an empire: Sargon of Akkad. It has eight charges and the wearer may cast the spells below by expending the necessary charges. The spell save DC is 17. The crown regains 1d6 expended charges daily at dawn.
• command (1 charge) • detect thoughts (2 charges) • silence (2 charges) • dominate person (5 charges)

properties.name: Description
properties.value: This belt is studded with gems and inlaid with golden writings of holy scriptures, and lion carvings. It inspires courage in the wearer. The wearer is immune to the frightened condition as long as it is attuned to this girdle.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: This two-handed weapon can expel flames in a 20 ft. line. The creatures inside the area must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 14). On a failed save, they take 4d8 fire damage. The weapon has 5 charges and every attack expends 1 charge. The flamethrower regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
This cane once belonged to Hacivat, the famous Shadowactor who is rumored to have magically managed to survive as a shadow to this very day. Hacivat, as a jester and actor, lived in the 13th century, during the invasion of Anatolia and the rapid Islamization of Turks. This cane still contains some piece of his power. All Charisma checks while wielding this cane are made with advantage. Any use of Bardic Inspiration will also have advantage. The cane has 12 charges and can be used to cast the spells below:
• vicious mockery (at will) • charm person (1 charge) • disguise self (1 charge) • detect thoughts (2 charges) • suggestion (2 charges) • hypnotic pattern (3 charges) • fear (3 charges) • hallucinatory terrain (4 charges)
Also, the wielder of the cane is immune to any mind-altering illusion, phantasm, enchantment or charm magic. The cane gives +2 AC and advantage on all spell saving throws while the wielder is holding the cane.
The charges of the cane only recover after another wielder attunes to the cane. It will not attune to any previous users who expended all the uses of the cane.

properties.name: Attunement Requirement
properties.value: by a cleric or a druid

properties.name: Description
properties.value: This was once a very strong and potent magical item; now it only holds a small amount of power left. There are 3 charges in the staff, each charge can be either used as a cure wounds or a lesser restoration spell. Cure wounds can be cast up to 3rd level by expending more charges. After all the charges are expended, the staff can still be used as a magical weapon. The staff regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Sometimes janissaries of legendary marksmanship were given well decorated muskets with magically enhanced firepower. They are quite rare and require the extensive training of a janissary to be used. When attuned, this musket gives the ability to overcome piercing resistance to the one using it. Only creatures with proficiency in martial weapons can use this musket.

properties.name: Attunement Requirement
properties.value: by one creature who is proficient in martial weapons

properties.name: Description
properties.value: These circular shields were awarded to prominent janissaries for their valor and courage in combat. There are many different designs but all of them have the same ability. The shield grants the janissary resistance against any one type of damage once a day as a reaction. This ability recharges at dawn.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: This kopis is far from the weapon it is meant to be, with its frail steel and cracked hilt. However, it wields immense authority and magical powers. It is rumored to be the Kopis of Alexander, found in a ditch near the easternmost border of the Ottoman empire, in the foot of the Zagros Mountains. Once attuned, it grants the owner proficiency on Persuasion and Intimidation checks. If the owner already has proficiency in either of these, it grants expertise instead. It also gives advantage on any Charisma check performed by the wielder.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: These coins were passed from generation to generation and were first minted and used by the Lydians, who were the founders of minted currency. These coins, originating in Anatolia and used throughout all the civilizations in this geographical area, are a powerful symbol of trading fairly. Although they do not have any magical powers, it is through their cultural significance that they give the owner a fully fair trade in one trade agreement. The seller won't have ill intentions to rip off the buyer in this said trade.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: This magic mirror once belonged to Hurrem Sultan, as the mirror carries her old name. It can be activated with the command word: доверять ('Doveryat'), which means trust. The first person looking into the mirror must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 14). On a failed save the person who has spoken the command word can manipulate the mind of a creature that has looked into the mirror, as if it were under the effects of the suggestion spell. The affected creature will believe and regard as the truth the next sentence it will hear from the speaker of the command word.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
Also known as “Musqa” by commoners, these talismans are crafted by clerics or, most often than not, by charlatans. Most do not do anything, but some actually have arcane properties.
If the players come across a working Musqa, any saving throws against curses or possession effects targeting the player are rolled with advantage. The wearer is always under the effect of a protection from evil and good spell and also can cast bless spell once a day. This ability recharges at dawn.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: This rose colored potion smells like cinnamon and has a sweet taste. When drunk, it gives advantage on all Charisma checks for 8 hours.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: This longbow is designed to use while on horseback. While you are mounted, it gives +2 to damage rolls.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: This ring stores spells cast into it, holding them until the attuned wearer uses them. The ring can store up to 5 levels worth of spells at a time. When found, it contains 1d6-1 levels of stored spells chosen by the GM. Any creature can cast a spell of 1st through 5th level into the ring by touching the ring as the spell is cast. The spell has no effect, other than to be stored in the ring. If the ring can’t hold the spell, the spell is expended without effect. The level of the slot used to cast the spell determines how much space it uses. While wearing this ring, you can cast any spell stored in it. The spell uses the slot level, spell save DC, spell attack bonus, and spellcasting ability of the original caster, but is otherwise treated as if you cast the spell. The spell cast from the ring is no longer stored in it, freeing up space. The wielder also has advantage on all Charisma saving throws.

properties.name: Attunement Requirement
properties.value: by a bard, cleric, or a druid

properties.name: Description
properties.value: This drum is the instrument of a shaman. It is used by shamans to worship Tengri, the Sky God of the Steppes. It can be used as a spellcasting focus. When attuned, the drum maximizes the benefit of spending a hit dice once per person during a short rest. This also includes the song of rest ability of the bard.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
Bronze mirrors are said to contain mystic powers in ancient civilizations. According to legends, one certain mirror was enchanted during the Warring States period in China by the lady Chang’e, goddess of the moon. It was lost during the Mongolian invasion.
The holder of this mirror has the following benefits while attuned to it:
• The holder has advantage on saving throws against body altering spells and magical effects, such as petrifying gaze or polymorph spells. This property can’t be used again until next dawn.
• The holder can cast protection from evil and good spell without expending a spell slot but can’t do so again until the next dawn.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
This spear is a Magic Weapon. When you hurl it and speak its Command word, it transforms into a piercing and burning light, forming a line 5 ft. wide that extends out from you to a target within 60 ft. Each creature in the line excluding you and the target must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 13), taking 4d6 fire damage on a failed save, and half as much damage on a successful one. The piercing light turns back into a spear when it reaches the target. Make a ranged weapon attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes damage from the spear plus 4d6 fire damage. The spear’s property can’t be used again until the next dawn. In the meantime, the spear can be used as a Magic Weapon.
The command word is “Jatiy”, the Achaemenid word meaning “to strike.” It was found near the Taurus Mountains, hinting that this spear was used against Alexander the Great during the Battle of Issus.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: By using these spectacles, you can avoid directly making eye-contact with the creatures thanks to the cleverly installed and magically enhanced mirrors. While wearing the glasses, the creatures are unaffected by petrification effects, such as those from a Medusa’s gaze.

entities.name: Sword of Yakup Agha of the Janissary Hearth

properties.name: Description
properties.value: This magical longsword has +2 bonus to hit and damage rolls. While attuned to it, the user is proficient with Wisdom saving throws.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Common folk call this the “Nazar Charm” in Turkish, and it protects the wearer against ill effects. When the wearer fails a saving throw against a curse or an enchantment spell, the talisman is broken, and the saving throw is considered a success. A person can benefit only once from this item in one given day.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: This blade is said to be used by Solomon, the Sultan of Djinns. It was taken by Selim the Grim from Solomon’s tomb in the Holy Lands and was brought to Anatolia. Suleiman the Magnificent is rumored to have also wielded this blade, too. The blade is a +2 Yatagan, dealing an extra 2d6 psychic damage against any non-Djinns. The wielder of the blade can see any Djinns whether they are in their Form of Al Ghaib or not, and is immune to any possession attempt.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: This turban is made from the finest white silk and adorned with an exquisite line of peridots. It grants the wielder proficiency in History skill; if the wielder already has proficiency in History skill, it gives expertise instead. The turban also gives the ability to cast comprehend languages once a day. This ability recharges at dawn.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: This mechanical contraption can be worn over any armor and grants the attuned person a 60 ft. flight speed for 10 minutes. The wings have only one charge, which recharges each dawn.

properties.name: Attunement Requirement
properties.value: by a Wizard, Sorcerer or a Warlock

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The first of these arcane tomes were written by the famous poet Ahmad Yasevi and throughout the ages, many copies were made by strong spellcasters. This tome can be used by spellcasters to exchange their vitality to perform more magic. The owner of this tome can use an action to sacrifice 5 hit points per spell level to regain expended spell slots three times a day. The tome can be used to regain up to 3rd level spells. The hit points of the caster can never go below 0 hit points to gain spell slots and temporary hit points can’t be used to regain spell slots.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Uses the stats of a dagger, with the exception that it can also deal slashing damage. Range 20/60.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Uses the stats of a scimitar.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Uses the stats of a shortsword.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Uses the stats of a longsword.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Uses the stats of a halberd.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Uses the stats of a Greataxe.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Uses the stats of a greatsword.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Uses the stats of a spear. Range 20/60.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Uses the stats of a lance.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Uses the stats of a javelin. Range 20/60.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Range 200/500. Gunpowder: if you roll the maximum amount of damage in the damage die, reroll the die and add it to the resulting damage.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Range 200/500. Gunpowder: if you roll the maximum amount of damage in the damage die, reroll the die and add it to the resulting damage.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Range 350/700. Gunpowder: if you roll the maximum amount of damage in the damage die, reroll the die and add it to the resulting damage.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Range 25. Gunpowder: if you roll the maximum amount of damage in the damage die, reroll the die and add it to the resulting damage.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: There are bruisers who dwell in cities who command respect among the common folk and other warriors alike. Their words respected, their deeds valued, their stern demeanor feared. They roam the streets and right wrongs, their own way. They know the code and creed of their city, just as they wrote it themselves. An urban vagabond is a charismatic drifter, who has his own understanding of justice and injustice, law and fear in society. They are upheld by the people and they shine where the local guards fail to do so. Their shouts in combat inspire their allies, and dissolve the fighting spirit of their adversaries. They use their natural charisma in order to taunt and psychologically disarm enemies.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A majestic festival scene in the gold-blue nights of Istanbul, and at the heart of the field, crowds gather to see their favorite show: the art of shadowacting. A candle lights up a beige canvas scene, elaborate puppets and gestures cast shadows, of human and beast, of city and the wild. The master of these shadowy figures, the bard, plays with them, twists and turns their blackened image, telling fascinating tales of joy and deceit, captivating hundreds gathered around. One time, my curiosity got the better of me. I snuck up to the backstage to see what was happening there. No one believed me when I told this story but I swear, I saw no one moving the puppets...Well, no puppets at all. Only a candle shining bright - with shadows cast, from the shimmering flames themselves. My eyes locked with the flame and smoke dancing on top of the candle - shimmering sparks and embers, and I lost touch with reality. Just before I was about to fall headfirst from the platform, I saw the shadow actress, emerging from the shadows in the canvas itself to catch me. She was such a lovely lady…

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Magic is unstable. Cast a potent spell, and you risk cracking open the reality and letting loose horrors to our mortal realm. There are those devoted to serving reality itself and soothe the pain of the universe plagued by chaos. They wander the realms, aiming to prevent the cracks from appearing, mending those already broken and keeping reality stable for all sentient life. Their motivations may come from a higher calling such as religion, or from their own will to serve the reality itself. They are adept at hunting down mages, discovering origins of arcane, and curing spellborne wounds.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Nazar, ward of the evil eye. It is the common people's belief that the blue beads with eyes drawn on them protect from those with bad intentions. They do not know that it is not the beads that protect, but those who create and control them. Circle of Nazar Druids seek to protect ordinary people from unintended magical effects or intended evil from charms and enchantments. They roam the wilderness, ruins and the cities to seek out curses, ill-intents, evil arcane, with the help of beads with the sign of the Nazar.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
A victorious soldier emerging from the hellish battleground with a proud face and blood taints on his armor. He is not a simple warrior that was levied on the eve of a war. He was trained as a soldier for years and years and honed a military tradition since the days of Caesars in the west and Khagans in the east. His profession is warfare, he guards stability and security during the times of peace and does his jobs as a warrior in the times of war. He is a Janissary that knows and excels in fighting.

Many Ottoman conquests owed their success to the discipline and mastery of the Janissaries. Taken as children from Ottoman-ruled Christian lands, and trained since childhood as elite imperial soldiers, the Janissary are masters of commanding a diverse range of weaponry in a single combat. Armed to the teeth with a large selection of weapons, their prowess shines in the battlefield as they utilize all in a bewildering flurry of gunpowder, arrow, bolt and blade.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: One hand looks upwards: “Take wisdom from heaven”; one hand faces the ground “...and share it with the Earth.” Start the whirl-like dance, spin as the Earth does; to lose thyself, and to find thyself. Some monks seek enlightenment through combining a trancelike state of chanting and whirling to sever their link to reality. Yet these actions are not just a way of escaping the mundane world. Their martial skill is immense, thanks to their impeccable balance, and unorthodox fighting stance. They call forth divine boons for their companions, and crack their foes’ stance and posture with their grappling attacks.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Some words need not be said. There are times where an oath of silence must be taken to protect a secret, or your sanity, or to channel a spectacular judgment. In either way, these warriors use the unsaid words to fuel their warfighting and instead use telepathy and thaumaturgy for communication. Their oaths are taken, usually, to fight against those who mislead and lie to their people; in some cases, those who take this oath usually face unimaginable horror and decide to speak no more, and in others, these are philosophers who believe that oft times words are misunderstood, but actions are not.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
A woman with a perfect dress, subtly moves and listens to an important conversation between two ambassadors of great presidents and kings. While she is undetected by others, successfully retrieves a document that was not meant for her eyes.

A young man with ragged clothes appears as a kindly flower-seller in the streets while carefully collecting information on the influence of a certain organization among the common folk. Slowly, he connects the dots and prepares his deadly strike for the necessary enemy.

Every government worth their salt has uses for people who do the dirty damning deeds that should never come to light. These individuals– whether trained for their job professionally or just happened to be the one person for the job– can charm, stab, or sneak their way into and out of different situations as a true jack of all trades

- or by seeming like jacks of all trades.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Even a whisper of their name may put your soul in danger. And if you try to bend them to your will, you should not expect to walk away. Djinns, hungry and curious of this world-plane, try every trick in the book to break from the chains of their usually overconfident masters. Once tricked, or with their will broken, masters' bodies become the playground of the Djinn. In moments of clarity, Djinnpossessed can act as their own masters, using their memory and knowledge of the world. However, most often, they act as their own in a foreign body and a foreign realm, trying to absorb as much information as possible, and trying to blend in. Yet their inexperience on Earth is quite easy to catch. In combat, they let loose all their abilities, using the body they host not as an object of tremendous value to be protected, but as an expendable weapon. Their arcane powers usually focus on this stratagem.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Sages of old devoted their waking lives to the pursuit of wisdom and knowledge, and even in death, they continue to guide their predecessors. Speaking through the artifacts they leave, their lessons and powers are echoed within those who seek and excel through knowledge of ancient arts. Their students gain abilities in acquiring information, detecting the supernatural, and at their epitome; they can summon the spirits of the dead from their belongings at will to converse with those who are long gone.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: It is told that they are made of smokeless fire, a primordial substance. Calling them elementals would be wrong; what we call smokeless fire has nothing to do with our version of flames. Their need for a vessel is also a myth, neither do they grant any wishes. But, it is true that they scour a parallel world, and they constantly interfere with ours. Never call them by their name, names have the power to summon - refer to them as “three lettered ones” instead. Contacting them is fruitless, and not because they won’t answer. They will. But you risk them having a laugh at the expense of your bodily integrity. Worse is if one takes a liking to you. They are envious creatures, and god almighty help those that they think are a competition for your attention. There are ways to keep them in line, yes. And of course those dark techniques can be taught. But have you considered what you will unleash if you happen to lose control? How will you keep the balance with your partners from the other realm, once you invite them to your body?

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Gazing at the stars is an ancient art form, almost as old as history itself. Those with the arcane skills and knowledge have used the essence of the stars and their movements to enhance the potency of their foresight. The stargazers, with their understanding of time and fate, excel in manipulating the destinies of people around them.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
Thanks to extensive practice with firearms, you gain the following benefits:

• You ignore the loading quality of firearms with which you are proficient.

• Being within 5 feet of a hostile creature doesn't impose disadvantage on your ranged attack rolls.

• When you use the Attack action and attack with a one-handed weapon, you can use a bonus action to attack with a one-handed repeating gun you are holding.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
When you gain this feat, your inner-eye will awaken, and you will get the ability to see glimpses into possible futures. Your Wisdom score increases by 1.

You can cast augury once a day, without expending a slot but only as a ritual. You can use marble-mugs, someone else’s hands, tarot cards, arrow heads, or any other cultural fortune-telling items as your material component. By casting it this way augury chooses a target, instead of an event, that will take place 30-minutes in the future. The GM may provide some omens and crypted messages about the future of the target of this spell, e.g. love life, death, birth, opportunities, wealth, family, friends, politics, failures, treachery, and more. Usually these omens are highly obscure and rarely a direct prophecy.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
You gain the following benefits:

• Increase your Intelligence or Wisdom score by 1.

• You gain the Survival or Investigation skill. If you are already proficient in the skill, you double your proficiency bonus for that skill.

• When you take the search action or make a Survival check to analyze clues and tracks in an urban area, you can learn the creature’s type.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
Your breath is considered one of the strong breaths that have healing properties among society. You gain the following benefits:

• You can cast exorcize djinni or remove curse once a day, without using a spell slot. You can’t do so again until you finish a long rest.

• You learn the purifying breath or guidance cantrips.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
You were once in contact with a djinn. This event affected you in the following ways:

• You learn evil eye, knot the luck, or viscious mockery cantrips.

• You can cast nazar or bestow curse once a day, without using a spell slot. You can’t do so again until you finish a long rest.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A life of chase, of high octane fights, and the thrill of bringing back your prey alive or dead; getting paid handsomely, and doing this for a living–You are a bounty hunter. You stalk your prey, prepare the best trap for the occasion, and strike your mark, with blades, chains, and all that is in your arsenal.

properties.name: Overview
properties.value:
How to Play?

Accept a bounty and you will be expected to turn in your target; either subdued somehow or as a corpse. A bounty hunt has four exciting steps: tracking, preparing, confronting, and bringing back the bounty. Each step is ladened with high danger, so make sure you are ready for this way of life!

Climbing the Ranks

For each bounty you turn in, you gain PEX (Profession Experience), and when you gather enough PEX, you will advance in the ranks of the bounty hunters. When you reach Rank 2 as a bounty hunter, you will have to be specialized in one way of hunting.

properties.name: Aspiring Background
properties.value: You decide to spend your life as a bounty hunter, and by taking this background, you can start this profession at level 1 with 0 PEX as an Apprentice Bounty Hunter.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A life of crime under the cover of darkness, and honing your skills in the art of thievery, extortion, breaking & entering, and swindling–Thieves, bandits, swindlers, prowlers, and robbers of all kinds fall under this profession. You are a burglar and earning your pay from the works of others is a day-to-day act for you.

properties.name: Overview
properties.value: How to Play? Decide on a mark, study how the mark acts during your free time. Find a weak or a sore spot and exploit it. Turn in the loot at a fence. Yet there will always be the forces of law trying to prevent you from getting your hard-earned gold. Be ready, be vigilant: it is not an easy life! Climbing the Ranks: For each act of thievery you perform, you gain PEX (Profession Experience) and when you gather enough PEX, you will advance in the ranks of the burglars. When you reach Rank 2 as a burglar you will have to be specialized in one way of theft. You earn PEX according to the gold pieces you earn from any type of heist. Each 10 gold pieces worth of stolen and fenced items will earn you 1 PEX.

properties.name: Aspiring Background
properties.value: You decide to spend your life as a burglar, and by taking this background, you can start this profession at level 1 with 0 PEX as an Apprentice Burglar. Proficiencies + Equipment. Skill Proficiencies: Stealth, Sleight of Hand. Languages: Any one of your choice. Tool Proficiencies: Thieves Tools. Equipment: Thieves Tools, a dark cloak, a crowbar and a pouch with 10 gp.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Being an Alchemist A calm and stoic life with a chance to improve yourself on a daily basis, honing your skills of making potions, elixirs, and concoctions–The hard work of an alchemist never ends.

properties.name: Overview
properties.value:
How to Play? As an alchemist, you will need to gather ingredients for your potions; then you will need to mix them in particular ways. The goal is to sell them to your buyers, earn some money and start it from the top.

Climbing the Ranks: For each potion you create, you gain PEX (Profession Experience) and when you gather enough PEX you will advance in the ranks of alchemists. When you reach Rank 2 as an alchemist, you will have to be specialized in the way of alchemy. You earn PEX according to the potions you create. Each potion will give you different amounts of PEX according to rarities.

Cooking Potions, A General Rule Anything an alchemist creates is considered a consumable. For every day of downtime you spend crafting, you can craft one or more items with a total market value not exceeding 25 gp, and you must expend raw materials worth half the total market value. If something you want to craft has a market value greater than 25 gp, you make progress every day in 25-gp increments until you reach the market value of the item. For example, a dose of poison of adder’s bite (market value 400 gp) takes 16 days to craft by yourself.

Multiple characters can combine their efforts toward the crafting of a single item, provided that the characters all have proficiency with the requisite tools and are working together in the same place. Each character contributes 25 gp worth of effort for every day spent helping to craft the item.

While crafting, you can maintain a modest lifestyle without having to pay 1 gp per day, or a comfortable lifestyle at half the normal cost.

properties.name: Aspiring Background
properties.value:
You decide to spend your life as an alchemist, and by taking this background, you can start this profession at level 1 with 0 PEX as an Apprentice Alchemist. Proficiencies + Equipment

Skill Proficiencies: Arcana, Nature

Languages: Any one of your choice.

Tool Proficiencies: Alchemist’s Supplies.

Equipment: Alchemist’s Supplies, a set of quality clothes, a carving knife, a scroll case full of parchment, a vial of ink, 10 gp.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Being an Antiquarian The life of a scientist who is versed in folklore and legends is never easy. Being a historian, a collector and an arcane researcher is both fulfilling and fruitful. The joy of providing prosperity with actual information about the properties of magic, wonders of enchanted items, and the vast world of magical research is, in itself, a very fulfilling job.

properties.name: Overview
properties.value:
How to Play? Find a dungeon, gather magical objects and non-magical historical objects, and present them to your colleagues in the academy or the occult circles. Write papers, books, and articles about ancient cultures, foreign languages, wonders of magic, and items of power.

Climbing the Ranks For each magical item you discover from old ruins, dungeons, or lost cities, you will gain PEX (Profession Experience) and when you gather enough PEX, you will advance in the ranks of the antiquarians. When you reach Rank 2 as an antiquarian you will have to be specialized in one way of an antiquarian.

properties.name: Aspiring Background
properties.value:
You decide to spend your life as an antiquarian and by taking this background you can start this profession at level 1 with 0 PEX as an Apprentice Antiquarian. Proficiencies + Equipment

Skill Proficiencies: Arcana, History

Languages: Any two of your choice.

Tool Proficiencies: None.

Equipment: A book scribbled with notes, a bottle of ink, an ink pen, traveler's clothes, a pouch containing 10 gp.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Being an Archeologist It is a common sight: adventurers delving deep into the ruins of ancient civilizations, derelict temples, and lairs of forgotten evils. Yet those ruins, temples, and lairs also contain important historical information that will help scientists and future generations to study and understand the past. Your mission as an archeologist is to preserve the ruins and artifacts for the future, and learn important skills by doing so.

properties.name: Overview
properties.value:
How to Play? The life of an archeologist is a life of danger for the sake of learning. You will be able to study and search for artifacts of the near and distant past and find out about how people lived in earlier civilizations and societies. An archeologist's career is spent in the lab and the field equally, writing reports and looking for treasures that other people may neglect, or worse, damage.

Climbing the Ranks For each artifact of the past, you recover, and for each derelict or ruin you restore, you gain PEX, and you advance in the ranks of archeologists. When you reach Rank 2 as an archeologist, you will have to be specialized in one way of recovery. You earn PEX according to the rarity of the artifact or derelict item you restore. The GM is the final judge on the rarity of the artifacts and derelicts you restore as well as the time required to restore a derelict or a relic. Archeologists gain PEX from two sources: Small and instant gains of PEX from discovering and restoring Relics and larger PEX gains from restoring Derelicts that take longer to restore.

properties.name: Aspiring Background
properties.value:
You decide to spend your life as an archeologist and by taking this background you can start this profession at level 1 with 0 PEX as an Apprentice Archeologist.

Proficiencies + Equipment

Skill Proficiencies: History, Survival.

Languages: Any one of your choice.

Tool Proficiencies: Cartographer's Tools or Navigator's Tools.

Equipment: A notebook, scroll case full of parchment, a bottle of ink, an ink pen, traveler's clothes, a lantern, a shovel, a one person tent, trinkets from a dig site (worth 5 gp), a pouch containing 15 gp.

Personality Trait (d8):
1. I am more at home when I am in a deserted city than a civilized one.
2. I might be a gentle person at heart, but I won't back down from a fight either.
3. I am not forgiving towards the people who damage priceless artifacts.
4. Some people might call me a tomb raider or a corpse robber, but I have no problem with taking stuff from the dead. They don't need those items anymore.
5. I collect trinkets and gimmicks from everywhere I go.
6. Failing is an essential for growing up; trying again is essential for success.
7. Puzzles, enigmas and mysteries are my forte; I love them with passion.
8. I would like to be well-known in academic circles.

Ideal (d6):
1. Protection. The artifacts and trinkets I uncover belong to a secure museum for the posterity to experience. (Good)
2. Peril. I have to endure great peril to discover something of great value. Hard work and danger are the different sides of the same ancient gold piece. (Any)
3. Knowledge. By understanding lost cultures and peoples, we can shed a light to the workings of the society of our day. (Neutral)
4. Obligation. It is our duty to bring light to the illiterate and the ignorant. (Lawful)
5. Timelessness. All my troubles and hard work are to gain immortality in the eyes of academic circles. (Any)
6. Ambition. I wish to seek power in my path and through my path I wish to see myself in a place of authority. (Evil)

Bond (d6):
1. I dedicate any items with a historical significance and rarity to a museum instead of using them for monetary gains.
2. I am dedicated to my work but someone, an apprentice or someone who works under me is in love with me. I know of this but don't know how to act towards this person.
3. I wish to earn my place in the academic circles.
4. I am trying to find the lost derelict from an ancient time. I want to learn of its secrets, and take my place in the dusty pages of history books.
5. I have a heinous rival trying to beat me in every aspect of our work. Only one of us can be the best and I plan to be the best.
6. I had a mentor, a friend or a loved one following the trails of a lost civilization but that person is lost now. I am trying to find this person.

Flaw (d6):
1. I have no roots. I am always on the move. I travel from one dig to another.
2. I have a quirk that gives me away when I am lying.
3. I am secretly claustrophobic. This makes me nervous when I am in a dungeon or a derelict.
4. I am awkward with people. I feel better when I am alone or in a dig site.
5. I act like a tyrant when I am on a dig site, ordering people around loudly…Very loudly.
6. Traps make me so insecure that I unnecessarily slow things.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Learn, whisper, and get paid. These souls of intrigue and espionage work on the act of obtaining confidential secrets from documents, people, or their very eyes. The work of a spy is usually part of larger institutions and governments, and it is through feeding secret information to their handler that they rise in their chosen career.

properties.name: Overview
properties.value: Infiltrate and sneak behind the enemy lines and get a hold of their secrets; then sell it to your enemies, or to the highest bidder; or rather you can work for a patron to serve under a great cause to save a nation from the oppressing forces. In your trade, the tastiest commodities are knowledge and secrets. For each secret you uncover and turn into some kind of profit, you gain PEX and advance in the ranks of spies. When you reach Rank 2 as a spy, you will have to be specialized in one way or agent craft. You earn PEX according to the secrets you uncover and deliver. The GM is the final judge on the rarity of the secrets you uncover.

properties.name: Aspiring Background
properties.value:
You decide to spend your life as a spy and by taking this background you can start this profession at level 1 with 0 PEX as an Apprentice Spy.

Proficiencies + Equipment

Skill Proficiencies: Choose two from among Deception, Insight, Persuasion and Stealth.

Languages: Any one of your choice.

Tool Proficiencies: Any one of your choice.

Equipment: A few sets of clothes appropriate to your duties and a pouch containing 25 gp.

entities.name: Bostanji-Bashi Ibrahim of the Black Hells

properties.name: Name
properties.value: Bostanji-Bashi Ibrahim of the Black Hells

properties.name: Skills
properties.value: Arcana +7, History +7, Investigation +7, Nature +7, Religion +7, Insight +7, Perception +7, Persuasion +6

properties.name: Damage Resistances
properties.value: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks, psychic.

properties.name: Senses
properties.value: Passive Perception 8, Tremore Sense 60 ft. blind beyond this point

properties.name: Damage Resistances
properties.value: radiant bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks.

properties.name: Senses
properties.value: Blindsight 120 ft. Darkvision 120 ft. Passive Perception 26

properties.name: Legendary Actions Intro
properties.value: Abra can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. Abra regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

properties.name: Damage Resistances
properties.value: bludgeoning, piercing and slashing from nonmagical weapons.

properties.name: Damage Resistances
properties.value: bludgeoning, piercing and slashing from nonmagical attacks, necrotic.

properties.name: Skills
properties.value: Acrobatics +10, Sleight of Hand +10, Stealth +10, Perception +2

properties.name: Skills
properties.value: Acrobatics +11, Intimidation +5 Perception +3 Sleight of Hand +11, Stealth +11

properties.name: Damage Resistances
properties.value: Bludgeoning, Piercing and Slashing from nonmagical attacks.

properties.name: Damage Resistances
properties.value: necrotic, bludgeoning, piercing and slashing from nonmagical weapons.

properties.name: Damage Resistances
properties.value: acid, fire, lightning, thunder, bludgeoning, piercing and slashing from nonmagical attacks.

properties.name: Condition Immunities
properties.value: exhausted, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained.

properties.name: Damage Resistances
properties.value: piercing, slashin and bludgeoning attacks from non-magical weapons.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A djinn may try to possess a creature within 5 ft. of it. The creature makes a Charisma saving throw. The DC is determined according to the djinn's Proficiency + Charisma bonus + 8. Some djinns may use a different stat for possession but saving throws are always Charisma based For example, a djinn with a proficiency bonus of 2 and a charisma of 16 will have the DC of 13. A creature must succeed on a DC13 to avoid possession.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
Although there is a spell called exorcize the djinni, some djinns are exceptionally powerful for such a spell. In these situations, a ritual must be performed by at least 2 individuals other than the possessed person in order to exorcize the djinn. The ritual takes 1 hour to perform. Whereabouts of the ritual must not be in total darkness: at least dim light is needed around the possessed body and the participants of the ritual.

Requirements:

• Talisman of Warding (Musqa)
• Holy Water
• A beloved keepsake of the possessed person
• The promise of a friend
• The blood of an enemy
• The touch of an innocent

Whoever leads the ritual must concentrate as if concentrating on a spell. The other participants must choose a way to contribute to the process of the exorcism: such as praying, offering a sacrifice, casting spells, or other ways. Then they roll an appropriate check. Here are some examples:

• Praying: Religion check.
• Performing a song or a poem: Performance check
• Casting a spell, such as protection from evil and good or bless: Spellcasting ability check.
• Offering a sacrifice: Persuasion check.

The GM must set a different difficulty for the ritual and these acts. The ritual's end difficulty is determined by the power of the djinn. Stronger djinns mean harder to exorcize.

After every successful check the end difficulty for the exorcism is lowered by 1, and by every failed check it is increased by 1. At the end of the ritual, the leader must roll a spellcasting ability check. If the person is not a spellcaster, they can use Wisdom.

For example, there's a djinn with an exorcizing DC 12, and a bard character performs a song about the great Solomon; they must succeed on a DC 10 Charisma (Performance) check. By succeeding, they reduce the difficulty for the exorcism by 1 (in this example from 12 to 11). The paladin offers his own trinket from a saint and must succeed on a DC 10 Persuasion check. On a success, DC is lowered to 10. The leader of the ritual, a wizard character, rolls a spellcasting ability check against DC 10. On a success, the djinn is exorcized.

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
Whenever a humanoid gets in direct contact with a djinn they will be under the effects of Djinnstruck. With an Intelligence (Arcana) check (DC 15) a creature may reverse the effects of the Djinnstruck. Also Lay on Hands (10), Lesser Restoration or powerful spells like Remove Curse, Greater Restoration also ends the curse.

Djinnstruck creatures will have:

• Impartial speech or speaking in Djinnspeak (GM's choice).
• Inability to control some of the basic muscle movements.
• Inability to control what they are saying. When they try to speak they will inevitably name one of the Djinnlord's name within a sentence.

Names of the Powerful Djinns

Shamhuresh
Nar-As Samum
Zaw'baw'ah
Abu Said
Abu Al Aswad
Abu Mihriz
Abu Al Harid

properties.name: Description
properties.value:
It is important to know a djinn's name, as they contain power. If you know a djinn's full and real name you may have some power on the djinn.

• When you utter the real name and a command sentence with it, the djinn must make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. If you don't have one, your Charisma modifier will be used to calculate one. On a failed save, the djinn will fulfill your command. On a matter of success (or after the compilation of your command) a djinn is immuned to this for the next 24 hours.
• Without issuing a command you may try to communicate with a djinn. When you utter their name and say nothing afterwards, the djinns are compelled to listen. Depending on the kind, you may strike a deal with them.

Some example djinn names (Name — Meaning):

• Ahger — Fire
• Maraz — Sickness, Trouble
• Farazi — Imagined
• Taran — Darkness
• Azihe — Lie
• Gufl — Unclear, uncertain
• Anber-nisar — The one that smells good
• Behajat — Beautiful
• Belagi — The one talks sweet.
• Asude — Silent
